claim_chain: We developed a hierarchical mathematical model using surveys and program data
7% of people aged ≥15 years had ever used an HIVST as of 2024
Males are more likely to use HIVST than females
About 70% of distributed kits are used
HIVST can play a role in closing HIV diagnosis gaps
Scale-up was highly variable across countries and regions

typical_pattern: Present supporting evidence with specific numbers

likely_priorities: Parameter identifiability and precision of estimates
Generalizability given exclusion of countries without program data
Temporal alignment between survey and program data
Model assumptions about kit usage timing
likely_attacks: Wide uncertainty in re-testing rate ratio and proportion of kits used
Potential recall bias and misclassification in self-reported data
Overestimation of uptake due to exclusion of countries without program data
Inability to model HIVST positivity and confirmatory testing
Assumption that kits are used in the same calendar year they were distributed
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly lists limitations including: 'some of the model parameters are best informed in settings with a good temporal overlap between survey and program data. However, few countries met this criterion, which led to imprecise estimates'; 'survey data on HIVST use are self-reported and could be subject to recall bias and/or misclassification'; 'we excluded six countries that did not have any HIVST program data. Countries that did not collect information on HIVST distribution could have lower HIVST usage, which would lead to overestimation'; 'we did not model HIVST positivity and awareness of HIV status'; and 'our analysis triangulating survey and program distribution data assumed that HIVST kits were used in the same calendar year they were distributed. This may be inaccurate in situations where kits are intentionally retained for later use.'

examples.quoted_phrase: Our results suggest that males are more likely to use an HIVST

This points to the potential role that HIVST can play in closing HIV diagnosis gaps

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. All numeric values appear in the input. No external references, author names, or invented data were included. The profile is based solely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legends.
